title: net/http Transport Ownership, Redirects, Bodies, and Timeouts · Execution Trace: Request Identity
summary: Every request creates a Client, redirects escape policy, response bodies leak on errors, Client.Timeout hides phase causes, and ReadAll ignores size. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go request identity increment in owned bounded net/http retrieval adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Build safe retrieval requests with context, honest User-Agent and contact, Accept, validators, method, credentials, and trace identity.
Configure and reuse one owned Transport with explicit proxy, dial, TLS, idle pool, compression, limits, and CloseIdleConnections policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6453 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense.
